heading: Create the MongoDB Search Indexes
body:
In this section, you can learn how to create a MongoDB Search index named `default` on
all the fields in the `companies` collection in the
`sample_training` database. You will create another MongoDB Search index
named `default` on all the fields in the `inspections`
collection in the `sample_training` database. You must perform the
following steps for each collection.

The following index definition dynamically indexes the fields of
[supported types](bson-data-chart) in the collection. You
can use the MongoDB Search Visual Editor or the MongoDB Search JSON
Editor in the Atlas user interface to create the index.

Visual Editor

Review the default index definition for the
collection.

JSON Editor

- Review the index definition.

  Your index definition should look similar to the
  following example:

  ```json
  {
    "mappings": {
      "dynamic": true
    }
  }
  ```

A modal window displays to let you know your index is building.
Click the Close button.

The index should take about one minute to build. While it is
building, the Status column reads `Build in
Progress`. When it is finished building, the
Status column reads `Active`.
